snippet.title: ショート動画｜Casual vs Text コンビニ1 1
snippet.description:
＼Casual vs Formal🔥／
at the convenience store🏪

Learn Japanese with me in 1 min(∩´∀｀)∩
For more videos, please follow me✨
@am_japanese 

✅ Next please
Casual: 次の人 (tsugi no hito)
Formal: お次でお待ちの方 (otsugi de omachi no kata)

✅ Welcome!
Casual: いらっしゃい (irasshai)
Formal: いらっしゃいませ (irasshaimase)

✅ Would you like it warmed up?
Casual: あっためる？ (attameru?)
Formal: 温めますか？ (atatamemasu ka?)

✅ Would you like a bag?
Casual: 袋いる？ (fukuro iru?)
Formal: レジ袋はご利用になりますか？ (reji bukuro wa go-riyou ni narimasu ka?)

✅ Would you like chopsticks/spoon?
Casual: お箸・スプーンいる？（ohashi supuun iru?)
Formal: お箸・スプーンお付けしますか？ (ohashi supuun otsuke shimasuka?)

✅ That will be 1,000 yen.
Casual: 会計 1000円ね (kaikei sen-en ne)
Formal: お会計は1000円でございます (okaikei wa sen-en de gozaimasu)

✅ Here is your change, 100 yen
Casual: 100円、おつり (hyaku-en otsuri)
Formal: 100円のお返しです (100-en no okaeshi desu)

I hope it’s helpful😊👍

snippet.title: Casual vs Textbook (Time⏰🗓️) #nihongo #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage #jlpt
snippet.description:
＼Casual vs Formal🔥／

✅ Today
Casual: 今日 (kyou)
Formal: 本日 (honjitsu)

✅ Tomorrow
Casual: 明日 (ashita)
Formal: 明日 (asu/ myounichi)

✅ Yesterday
Casual: 昨日 (kinou)
Formal: 昨日 (sakujitsu)

✅ The other day
Casual: この前 (kono mae)
Formal: 先日 (senjitsu)

✅ Now
Casual: 今 (ima)
Formal: 現在 (genzai)

✅ Earlier
Casual: さっき (sakki)
Formal: 先ほど (sakihodo)

✅ Later
Casual: あとで (ato de)
Formal: 後ほど (nochihodo)

✅ From now on / In the future
Casual: これから (korekara)
Formal: 今後 (kongo)

✅ Immediately
Casual: すぐに (sugu ni)
Formal: ただちに (tadachi ni)

✅ Soon
Casual: もうすぐ (mou sugu)
Formal: まもなく (mamonaku)

How many did you know?!😎
I hope it’s helpful😊👍

Don’t forget to save this post to review later😉
For more Japanese content🍣🍜
@am_japanese

snippet.title: How to say "You're kind" I'm poetic way🇯🇵 #japanese #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
Check out the captions, too👍✨

In English we say: You’re kind🔥
In Japanese Literature, we say

🌟あなたは、夜空(よぞら)を優(やさ)しく照（て）らす月（つき）の光（ひかり）のような人（ひと）です。
（anata wa yozora o yasashiku terasu tsukino hikari no youna hito desu.）
=You are like the gentle light of the moon, softly illuminating the night sky.

🌟春(はる)の陽(ひ)だまりのような温(あたた)かさを持(も)っていますね。
(haru no hidamari no youna atatakasa o motteimasune)
=You possess the warmth of a spring sunbeam.

🌟春(はる)の花（はな）がそっと咲（さ）くような方ですね。
(haru no hana ga sotto saku youna kata desune)
=You are like a spring flower, softly blooming.

The words "moon," "spring," and "flower" have a very gentle image in Japanese. ✨
Is it the same in your country? 😊

If you like this video, leave 💓 in the comment😊

#japanese #learnjapanese #nihongo #studyjapanese #jlpt #kanji #famousquotes

snippet.title: 水 is N5 kanji BUT...🤯 #learnjapanese #kanji #jlpt #japaneselanguage
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
水（water) has many different readings in Japanese😎

@am_japanese 

⭐ mizu
✅ 水（みず / mizu）
📝 meaning: water

✅ 飲み水（のみ-みず / nomi-mizu）
📝 meaning: drinking water

✅ 水着（みず-ぎ / mizu-gi）
📝 meaning: swimsuit

ーーーーーーー

⭐ sui
✅ 水曜日（すい-よう-び / sui-you-bi）
📝 meaning: Wednesday

✅ 水道（すい-どう / sui-dou）
📝 meaning: water supply

✅ 水泳（すい-えい / sui-ei）
📝 meaning: swimming

✅ 香水（こう-すい / kou-sui)
📝 meaning: perfume 

ーーーーーーー

⭐ zui
✅ 洪水（こう-ずい / kou-zui）
📝 meaning: flood

ーーーーーーー

⭐ special readings

✅ 水母（くらげ / kurage）
📝 meaning: jellyfish

✅ 手水（ちょうず / chouzu）
📝 meaning: ritual hand washing (at a shrine)

✅ 水無月（みなづき / minazuki）
📝 meaning: traditional name for June

漢字（かんじ）って大変（たいへん）だけど、おもしろいね！
みんながんばろう！💪🔥

snippet.title: Basic phrases in 3ways!😎✨ #learnjapanese #jlpt #studyjapanese
snippet.description:
🌟What is it？
Casual:何？(nani?)
Polite:何ですか？(nan desuka?)
Very Polite:何でしょうか？(nan deshouka?)

🌟Where is it?
Casual: どこ？(doko)
Polite：どこですか？(doko desuka?)
Very Polite:どちらでしょうか？(dochira deshouka?)

🌟When is it？
Casual:いつ？(itsu?)
Polite:いつですか？ (itsu desuka?)
Very Polite:いつでしょうか？ (itsu deshouka?)

🌟Who is it？
Casual:誰？(dare?)
Polite:誰ですか？(dare desuka?)
Very Polite:どちら様でしょうか？
(dochirasama deshouka?)

🌟Whose is this?
Casual: 誰の？(dareno?)
Polite:誰のですか？(dareno desuka?)
Very Polite:どなたのでしょうか？(donatano deshouka?)

🌟How？
Casual:どうやって？ (douyatte?)
Polite:どうやってですか？(douyatte desuka?)
Very Polite:どのようにでしょうか？(donoyouni deshouka?)

📝 Quick Tip!

To make a casual question polite, 
just add 「ですか」 to the end.
To make it very polite, use 「でしょうか」, but sometimes the words themselves change (e.g., "どこ" → "どちら", "誰の" → "どなたの").

I hope it’s useful😊👉



#Japanese #LearnJapanese #jlpt #japanesevocabulary #studyjapanese #JapaneseCulture #JapanLover #Nihongo #JapaneseLanguage #ExploreJapan #JapaneseLife #JapanTravel #JapaneseGrammar #日本語 #日語 #言語学習

snippet.title: ✨ All 9 Japanese Coins & Bills ✨
snippet.description:
Save for tripe✈🌸

⭐coins=小銭（kozeni)
🚀foraml way=硬貨（kouka)

✅ 1yen=1円（ichi en)
✅ 5yen=5円（go en)

⭐yen note=お札（osatsu)
🚀formal way=紙幣（shihei)

✅ 1000yen=千円（sen en)

snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
母(mother) has many different readings in Japanese😎

⭐haha

✅ 母（はは / haha）
📝meaning: mother(formal)

⭐kaa
✅ お母さん（お-かあ-さｎ / o-kaa-san）
📝meaning: mother (neutral way)

⭐bo
✅ 母音（ぼ-いん / bo-in）
📝meaning: Vowel

✅ 祖母（そ-ぼ / so-bo)
📝meaning: grandmother

✅ 母乳 (ぼ-にゅう / bo-nyuu)
📝meaning: breast milk

⭐ba
✅ 乳母（う-ば / u-ba）
📝meaning: wet nurse

✅ 伯母or叔母 (お-ば / o-ba)
📝meaning: aunt

⭐omo
✅ 母屋（おも-や / omo-ya）
📝meaning: It refers to the primary building of a property, especially when there are other smaller buildings (like annexes, guesthouses, or storage rooms) on the same land.

snippet.title: Many ways to say "Mom" in #japanese #learnjapanese #kanji #jlpt
snippet.description:
🗣️ Did you know there are many ways to say "Mom" in Japanese? 🇯🇵

🔸 お母さん (Okaasan): Neutral, commonly used by everyone in daily conversations to refer to their mother.

🔸 母上 (Hahaue): Extremely formal and respectful, often used historically or humorously nowadays, giving a dignified feeling.

🔸 ママ (Mama): Casual, affectionate, and often used by young children or those who prefer a softer, warmer tone.

🔸 あま (Ama): A special dialect from Tokunoshima, used affectionately to refer to one's mother, highlighting regional warmth and tradition.

🔸 おかあ (Okaa): Casual and intimate, frequently used within close-knit families to express familiarity and warmth.

🔸 母ちゃん (Kaachan): Casual and friendly, giving a cozy, approachable feel, often used affectionately in casual family conversations.

🔸 ママ子 (Mamako): Playful, slightly humorous or teasing way to call one's mother, indicating a close, fun relationship.

Have you heard any of these before?
Which one do you like the best?

But guess what—there are even more ways to call your mother in Japanese! 🌸

おふくろ、おっかあ、おっかさん、おかん・・・and more!

Follow for more Japanese contents 🗻

snippet.title: How many did you know??😎👍 #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage #jlpt #kanji
snippet.description:
How many「とる (toru)」did you know!?😎
Did you know 「とる (toru)」has many meanings in Japanese?😲

@am_japanese 

It’s not just one “take”!
Let’s explore its different uses!🙌

1⃣ 🧂 取る (take / pass) /toru/
ex. 塩を取る = pass the salt

2⃣ 📸 撮る (take a photo) /toru/
ex. 写真を撮る = take a photo

3⃣ 📝 取る (take / write down) /toru/
ex. メモを取る = take notes

4⃣ 🎩 取る (remove,take off) /toru/
ex. 帽子を取る = take off a hat

5⃣ ⚽ 捕る (catch) /toru/
ex. ボールを捕る = catch a ball

6⃣ 🍓 採る (pick / harvest) /toru/
ex. イチゴを採る = pick strawberries

7⃣ 🛵 取る (order) /toru/
ex. 出前を取る = order food delivery

8⃣ ⚖️ とる (Keep) /toru/
ex. バランスをとる = keep balance

9⃣ 🥗 摂る (intake) /toru/
ex. 栄養を摂る = intake nutrients

🔟 💰 盗る (steal) /toru/
ex. 財布を盗る = steal a wallet

1⃣1⃣ 📌 取る (take / accept) /toru/
ex. 責任を取る = take responsibility

🔤 All of these are pronounced “toru”, but the kanji and meanings change!

📚 Japanese verbs are deep and fascinating—let’s learn them step by step😉🇯🇵✨

Save this post for review🔖

Follow for more fun Japanese lessons👍
🍣🍜🌸🍵🗻👘

snippet.title: POV: How I Would Speak as Different Characters! Have you heard all of these? #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
POV: How I Would Speak as Different Characters!

＜English＞
Hi, how’s it going?
What are you up to?
How about grabbing some tea?

☑Normal 
「やっほー！元気？」
yahho genki?

「今、何してる？」
ima nanishiteru?

「お茶でもどう？」
ocha demo dou?

- - - - - - - - -

☑Kansai people
「おーー！ 元気？」
o~! genki?

「今、何してるん？」
ima nanishiterun?

「茶でもしばきに行かへん？」
cha demo shibaki ni ikahen?

- - - - - - - - -

☑Noble Lady
「まあ、ご機嫌よう。」
maa gokigenyou

「今、何をしていらっしゃるのかしら？」
ima nani o shiteirassharu nokashira?

「お茶でもご一緒しませんこと？」
ocha demo goissho shimasenkoto?

- - - - - - - - -

☑Samurai
「おう、達者であったか？」
ou tassha de attaka?

「今しがた何をしておる？」
imashigata nani o shiteoru?

「茶でも一服いたさぬか？」
cha demo ippuku itasanuka?

Have you heard all of these speaking styles?😎

snippet.title: How many readings of "東" do you know?😎✨🔥 #kanji #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
Special collab with @imari.japan.with_videos ✨

東(east) has many different readings in Japanese😎

✅ 東（ひがし / higashi）
📝meaning: east (direction)

✅ 東（あずま / azuma）
📝meaning: old/poetic word for eastern Japan (esp. Kanto region) or a Japanese family name

✅ 東（とう / tō）
Examples:
⭐ 東京（とうきょう / Tōkyō）= Tokyo
⭐ 東西南北（とうざいなんぼく / tōzai-nanboku）= east, west, south, north

✅ 東雲（しののめ / Shinonome） — special reading
Example:
⭐ 東雲駅（しののめえき / Shinonome-eki）= Shinonome Station, a station on the Rinkai Line in Tokyo

✅ 東風（こち / kochi） — special reading
Examples:
⭐ 東風（こち / kochi）= a Japanese family name

⭐ 東風吹かば 匂ひおこせよ 梅の花 主なしとて 春な忘れそ
(/kochi fukaba nioiokoseyo umenohana aruji nashi tote haruna wasureso/)
(Japanese old poem — waka by Sugawara no Michizane)
Meaning:
If the east wind blows,
send your fragrance, O plum blossoms
Though your master is gone,
do not forget the coming of spring.

みんながんばろう！💪🔥

snippet.title: Casual vs Polite(Restaurant ver.)🔥 #learnjapanese #jlpt #kanji #studyjapanese
snippet.description:
＼Casual vs Polite／
Polite:Restaurant ver🍽

✅ Hi✋
Casual: おー！（ooo!)
Polite: いらっしゃいませ(irasshaimase)

✅ How many people？
Casual: 何人？(nannin)
Polite: 何名様ですか？（nanmeisama desuka?)

✅ I got it.
Casual: オッケー（okkee)
Polite: かしこまりました（kashikomarimashita)

✅ Here’s your water.
Casual: はい、水 (hai mizu)
Polite: お冷です。(ohiya desu)

✅ Thank you! (We look forward to serving you again)
Casual: サンキュー (sankyuu)
Polite: ありがとうございました。またお越しくださいませ。
(arigatougozaimashita. mata okoshikudasaimase)

How many did you know?!😎

snippet.title: 馬(horse) is N4 kanji BUT with many readings...😲 #kanji #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage #jlpt
snippet.description:
N4 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
🐴馬(Horse) has many surprises in Japanese😎

⭐ uma
✅ 馬（うま / uma）
📝meaning: horse

⭐ ma (irregular)
✅ 絵馬（え-ま / e-ma）
📝meaning: wooden wishing plaque (at shrines)

⭐ ba
✅ 馬鹿（ば-か / ba-ka）
📝meaning: idiot / stupid

⭐ special readings
✅ 海馬（トド / todo）
📝meaning: sea lion (literally "Sea Horse"!)

⭐ same sound!
✅ 午（うま / uma）
📝meaning: horse (Chinese Zodiac sign)

snippet.title: How many did you know?✨ 4ways to say "Toilet" in #japanese #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
🚽4 Ways to Say 'Toilet' in Japaneseー Which One Do You Know?"✨

Here are 4 ways to say "toilet" based on the situation or tone!

1️⃣ Polite: お手洗い (おてあらい-otearai)

Example: お手洗いに行ってきます。
(otearai ni ittekimasu)
=I’m going to the restroom.

2️⃣ Casual: トイレ(toire)

Example: トイレ行ってくる。
(Toire ittekuru)

=I’m going to the toilet.

3️⃣ Manly: 便所 (べんじょ-benjo)

Example: 便所行ってくる。
(benjo ittekuru)

=I’m going to the toilet.

4️⃣ Part-time job slang: 4番 (よんばん-yonban)

Example: 4番行ってきます。
(yonban ittekimasu)

=I'm taking a #4 break." (Insider talk!)
In Japanese, "4番" (yonban) is used as a discreet way to refer to the toilet, especially in workplaces like part-time jobs. This avoids directly saying "toilet," which might be considered impolite or awkward if overheard by customers.

Which one will you use next? Let me know in the comments! 📝👇

#japanese #japaneselesson #japaneselanguage #learnjapanese #studyjapanese #nihongo

snippet.title: The Timeless Beauty of Japanese Culture✨ Please check the caption🙏✨ #japaneseculture
snippet.description:
Harmony with Nature: The Timeless Beauty of Japanese Culture✨

I know it’s too late to share but let me introduce one of the Japanese beautiful cultures that I fell in love in this summer🥰

Japanese wind chimes, or fūrin (風鈴), are hung in the summer to create soothing sounds that evoke a sense of coolness. 

Wind chimes are 「夏の風物詩(natsuno fuubutsushi), a symbol of summer in Japan.

Made from materials like glass or metal, their gentle ringing brings calm and relaxation, symbolizing harmony with nature.

 Fūrin are often hand-painted with seasonal designs and are believed to bring good luck.

 Their delicate sound and aesthetic beauty capture the fleeting moments of summer, reflecting Japan’s appreciation for nature and tranquility🎐

Let the melody of furin bring peace to your day. 🍃 How do you say 'wind chime' in your language? Share below! 🌸

snippet.title: Japanese Office Slang 😎👍 #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage #japaneselesson
snippet.description:
Do you think Business Japanese is complicated?! 💼🇯🇵

@am_japanese 

重要単語 ／ Important Vocabulary
☑ 焼く（やく）= 印刷する／コピーする（business slang）
☑ ボールを持つ = 担当する／リーダーになる
☑ たたき台 = ドラフト／粗案（draft / rough plan）
☑ 握る（にぎる）= クライアントとの関係をきちんと保つ
☑ 全員野球 = チーム全体で取り組むこと（teamwork）

＜台本／Script＞

👨🏻‍💼 ：これ10部(ぶ）、焼(や)いて。
(Please make 10 copies.)

👱‍♀️：や、やく？（焼く…？）
(Yaku? …like burn?)

👨🏻‍💼：うん、焼（や）いて。はやくはやく。
(Yeah, print them. Quickly.)

👱‍♀️：（ライターをとりだす）
(Takes out a lighter)

👨🏻‍💼 ：あーー焼（や）いたらダメ！コピーしてってこと！
(No, don’t burn it!! I mean “print it,” not “set it on fire”!)

👱‍♀️ Emma：Oh…

👨🏻‍💼 ：あ～そういえば、この前（まえ）のAMプロジェクト、
今（いま）ボール持（も）ってるの誰（だれ）？
(By the way, for the AM project—who has the ball now?)

👩🏻：今（いま）ボール持（も）ってるのはEmmaさんです。
(Emma is holding the ball now.)

👱‍♀️：え、ボール持（も）ってない…です。
(I’m not holding any ball…)

👨🏻‍💼：プロジェクトのリーダーってこと。
(It means you're the project lead.)

👱‍♀️：あ～、わたしがボール持（も）ってます。
(Ahh, I have the ball…)

👨🏻‍💼 ：じゃあ、たたき台(だい）、念（ねん）のため見（み）せて。
(Then, show me your draft, just in case.)

👱‍♀️：Tatakidai？like tataku(hit) and dai (table)?
(Okay…)

（机の“台”をたたく）
(hits the desk like a drum)

👨🏻‍💼 ：なんでやねん！！
(What are you doing!?)

👨🏻‍🏫 Note：
「たたき台(だい）」＝プロジェクトのドラフト／プラン
("Tatakidai" means draft/rough plan—not literally hitting a platform.)

👱‍♀️：I see…

👨🏻‍💼 ：大事（だいじ）なプロジェクトだから、
クライアントのこと、ちゃんと”握（にぎ）っといて”ね。
(It’s an important project. Make sure you properly “hold” the client.)

👱‍♀️：え？握（にぎ）る？寿司（すし）？🍣
(Hold… like sushi?)

👨🏻‍💼 ：いやいや、
クライアントと意見（いけん）をちゃんと合（あ）わせるってこと。
(No! It means “align with the client,” not making sushi.)

👱‍♀️：ビジネス日本語（にほんご）むずかしい…
(Business Japanese is so hard…)

👨🏻‍💼 ：全員野球（ぜんいん やきゅう）だよ！大丈夫（だいじょうぶ）！
(This is a team effort! We’ve got this!)

👱‍♀️：みんなで野球（やきゅう）？は？
(We play baseball all together? What?)

📝 Note：全員野球(ぜんいんやきゅう） = チームワーク、協力（きょうりょく）して進（すす）めること
(Means teamwork, not literally baseball)

snippet.title: 行 is N5 kanji, BUT....😎🔥 #kanji #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯

行 has many different readings in Japanese😎

☑ i (ku)
=To go

ex.)
学校に行く=To go to school
/Gakkō ni “i”ku/

☑ okona (u)
=To do something (formal)
ex.)
プレゼンを行う=To hold a presentation
/purezen o “okona”u/

☑ kō
ex.)
・旅行 (りょこう-ryo”kō”)=travel
・行動 (こうどう- “kō”dō)=action
・銀行（ぎんこう- gin”kō”）=bank

☑ gyō
ex.)
・行 (ぎょう-gyō)=line
・行事 (ぎょうじ-”gyō”ji)=event
・修行 (しゅぎょう-shu”gyō”)=ascetic training

☑ yu (ku)
ex.)
・行方 (ゆくえ-”yuku”e)=whereabouts
・行方不明(ゆくえふめい-”yuku”ehumei)=missing
・行く末 (ゆくすえ- “yuku” sue)=The future

☑ an 
ex.)
・行脚 (あんぎゃ-”an”gya)= pilgrimage, traveling campaign
・行灯（あんどん-”an”don）=traditional Japanese paper lantern

みんながんばろう！💪🔥

snippet.description:

✅ Please eat🍽

✅ Please drink🥛
Casual: 飲んで (nonde)
Polite: お飲みください（onomi kudasai)

✅ Please look👀
Casual: 見て (mite)
Polite: ご覧ください（goran kudasai)

✅ Please come👋
Casual: 来て (kite)
Polite: お越しください（okoshi kudasai)

✅ Please sleep💤🛏
Casual: 寝て (nete)
Polite: お休みください（oyasumi kudasai)

✅ Please take care🥺
Casual: 気を付けて (ki o tsukete)
Polite: お気を付けください（okiotsuke kudasai)

I hope it’s useful😊👍
For more Japanese contents, please follow me🫶
@am_japanese

snippet.title: How many "抜く(nuku)" do you know??😎 Please check the caption too👍 #learnjapanese #jlpt #kanji
snippet.description:
🙈😳🍆You should check the caption below😁

All sentences below use 「抜く(nuku)」
,but has different meanings!

①To skip
朝食を「抜く」
/choushoku o “nuku”/
=To skip breakfast

②To remove/pull out
まゆげを「抜く」
/mayuge o “nuku”/
=To remove eyeblow hairs

③To let something out/release
空気を「抜く」
/ kuuki o “nuku”/
=To let the air out
↑
For men, 「抜く」can mean something special… if you know, you know🍆🤫

④To surpass
ライバルを「抜く」
/ raibaru o “nuku”/
=To surpass a rival

I hope it’s usefull😉👍

snippet.title: Casual vs Formal（conjunction) #learnjapanese #jlpt #nihongo
snippet.description:
＼Casual vs Formal🔥／Conjunction ver.
More contents→ @am_japanese

✅ But
Casual: でも (demo)
Formal: ですが (desuga)

✅ Because
Casual: だって (datte)
Formal: なぜなら (nazenara)

✅ So
Casual: だから (dakara)
Formal: ですので (desunode)

✅ And
Casual: で (de)
Formal: そして (soshite)

✅ Then / Well then
Casual: じゃあ (jā)
Formal: それでは (soredewa)

✅ when～
Casual: ～とき (toki)
Formal: ～際 (sai)
ex.)when I go 
Casual 行くとき
Formal 行く際

How many did you know?!😎
I hope it's useful😊✨

snippet.title: Difference between いっぱい(ippai) and たくさん (takusan)😎👍 #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
＼Important👀‼／
What's the difference between
「いっぱい」 and 「たくさん」? 💡

Both can be translated as “a lot,"
but there's a subtle difference! 🚀

🌟いっぱい (ippai)=something is full or filled up

✅In kanji 一杯, so the image is the full of glass.
✅It can express more emotion.
✅It can be used more in casual talking.

Example:
・お菓子がいっぱいある
-okashi ga ippai aru
（sounds casual and excited)

・人がいっぱいいる
-hito ga ippai iru
（sounds casual and surprised/overwhelmed）

🌟たくさん (takusan)=many, plenty 
(but it doesn't have to be full.)

✅It’s easier to imagine when you write it in kanji: 沢山(たくさん）
It gives the image of things stacked up like a mountain⛰
（there’s a lot, but it doesn’t have to be full.）
✅It sounds calmer
✅Calm, objective, and explanatory

Example:
・お菓子がたくさんある（sounds more polite and calmer)
・人がたくさんいる（sounds more casual and calm）

So,いっぱい would be used more in daily conversation than たくさん😉

Thank you for watching!

#japanese #japaneselesson #japaneselanguage #learnjapanese #studyjapanese #nihongo #jlpt #japanesegrammar #日本語 #日本語学習

snippet.title: 木 is N5 kanji BUT with many readings🤯 #kanji #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
🌳木(tree/wood) has many different readings in Japanese😎

⭐ ki
✅ 木（き / ki）
📝meaning: tree / wood

⭐ moku
✅ 木曜日（もく-ようび / moku-youbi）
📝meaning: Thursday

⭐ boku
✅ 大木（たい-ぼく / tai-boku）
📝meaning: big tree

⭐ mo
✅ 木綿（も-めん / mo-men）
📝meaning: cotton

⭐ special readings

✅ 木偶（でく / deku）
📝meaning: wooden puppet / “dummy””useless” (as in 木偶の坊)

✅ 木乃伊（ミイラ / miira）
📝meaning: mummy

漢字っておくが深くて面白いよね🥺✨
みんながんばろう！💪🔥
I hope it’s useful👍

@am_japanese

snippet.title: Have you seen the netflix show called "Old Enough"?💡#japan #japaneseculture #learnjapanese
snippet.description:
Do you know the Netflix show called “Hajimeteno otsukai” (English title: Old enought)?✨

In Japan, children often go shopping by themselves to help their parents from a young age. 
This is called "otsukai."

My kids love going on ”Otsukai”.

If they have any problems, adults in the neighborhood often help them out.

It seems that meals after helping out taste extra delicious😋✨

Do kids run errands in your country?😊

snippet.title: "切れる(kireru)" means not only "cut" but has many other meanings!😎 #learnjapanese #jlpt #nihongo
snippet.description:
Checkout the captions to for more to learn！✨

Did you know 「切れる(kireru)」 has multiple meanings? not just “cut”?🤔
Let’s dive into its versatile uses!🙌

1⃣✂️ よく切れる (cut well)
/yoku kireru/

2⃣🚽 紙が切れる (toilet paper runs out)
/kami ga kireru/

3⃣🥫 賞味期限が切れる (expired)
/shoumikigen ga kireru/

4⃣🧠 集中が切れる (losing concentration)
/shuuchuu ga kireru/

5⃣💡 頭が切れる (sharp-minded, bright)
/atama ga kireru/

6⃣😡 キレる (lose one's temper, snap)
/kireru/

and more…!!!

📚 Keep learning and exploring the richness of the Japanese language together!😉 🇯🇵💪

Save this post so that you can review later🔖
And follow for more to learn Japanese♪
▶ @am_japanese_sushi_ramen

🍣🍜🌸🍵🗻👘

snippet.title: Polite😇 vs Pissed😡 ※Let's be polite🤣 #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
＼Polite✨ vs Pissed😡／

✅ You
Polite: (name)さん／(name)san
Pissed: おまえ (omae)

✅ Come here
Polite: こっちに来てください (kotchi ni kite kudasai)
Pissed: こっち来い (kotchi koi)

✅ Hurry up
Polite: 早くしてください (hayaku shite kudasai)
Pissed: 早くしろよ (hayaku shiro yo)

✅ Be quiet
Polite: 静かにしてください (shizukani shite kudasai)
Pissed: 黙れよ (damare yo)=Shut up

Let’s be polite🥰😉 lol

snippet.title: N5 kanji...BUT it's harrrrd..😅 #learnjapanese #kanji #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯

女 has many different readings in Japanese😎

☑ onna

ex.)
女(おんな-onna)=woman

☑ jo
ex.)
・女子 (じょし-joshi)=girl
・女性 (じょせい-josei)=woman
・女王 (じょおう-joou)=queen

☑ nyō, nyo
ex.)
・女房 (にょうぼう-Nyōbō)=wife
・天女 (てんにょ- tennyo)=celestial maiden

☑ me
ex.)
・乙女 (おとめ-otome)=meiden
・乙女座 (おとめざ-otomeza)=Virgo

☑ special reading
・海女=（あま-ama)=female free diver
・女将= (おかみ-okami)=Landlady,Madam Hostess
・巫女= (みこ- miko)=shrine maiden

みんながんばろう！💪🔥

snippet.title: 人 is one of the easiest kanji but one of the trickiest kanji that has many ways of reading😳 #kanji
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯

人 (person) has many different readings in Japanese😎

☑ hito
👤人(ひと-hito)–person 

☑ nin
👥何人(なんにん-nannin)–how many people 

☑ jin
🌍何人(なにじん-nanijin)
–what nationality

☑ ri　
🧍‍♂️ 一人(ひとり-hitori)–one person 

👨‍👩‍👧‍👦大人 (おとな-otona) – adult 
「大人」 (おとな), the reading is a special case where the whole word carries a specific meaning and pronunciation, rather than each kanji being read separately

☑ udo
🏹狩人(かりゅうど-karyuudo)–hunter


みんながんばろうね！💪🔥

snippet.title: Casual vs Formal🔥 part.2 #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt #japaneselanguage
6 diffrent "sleep" in #japanese 🇯🇵🛏️😴 #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
6 ways to say 'sleep'in Japanese😪🛏💤

Here's a quick breakdown to level up your vocabulary:

🌟寝る(neru):To go to bed

🌟二度寝する(nidone suru)：To go back to sleep

🌟爆睡する(bakusui suru)：To sleep deeply
or 熟睡する(jukusui suru) is also good👍

🌟寝たふりをする(netafuri o suru)：To pretend to be asleep or 噓寝する(usone suru) is also good!

🌟うたた寝する(utatane suru)：To doze off
or うとうとする(utouto suru) is also good👍

🌟寝落ちする(neochi suru)：To fall asleep

💡 Save this post and practice with me🙌

Follow me for more Japanese videos😉✨

#japanese #japaneselesson #japaneselanguage #learnjapanese #studyjapanese #nihongo

✅Stop it!
Anime: やめるんだ (yamerunda)
Real Life: やめて！(yamete)

✅I'm tired..
Anime: やれやれだぜ(yareyaredaze)
📝It can also mean “what a relief.”
Real Life:疲れた(tsukareta)

✅Foolish
Anime: 愚かな(orokana)
Real Life:馬鹿(baka)

✅What!?
Anime: なんだと!?(nandato)
Real Life:え!?(e)

✅Why?
Anime: なぜだ？なぜなんだ？（nazeda nazenanda)
Real Life:なんで？（nande)

✅OMG
Anime: なんてこった（nantekotta)
Real Life:まじか(majika)

✅Please help me.
Anime: 力を貸してくれ（chikara o kashitekure)
Real Life:手伝って(tetsudatte)

✅Who?
Anime: 誰だ？（dareda?)
Real Life:誰？（dare?)

✅Pretty good,huh!
Anime: なかなかやるな（nakanakayaruna)
Real Life:すごい（sugoi)

✅Bye
Anime: さらばだ（sarabada)
Real Life:じゃ（ja)

snippet.title: Essential words and phrases when you shop in Japan👖🇯🇵 #learnjapanese #jlpt #studyjapanese
snippet.description:
🛍️Shopping in Japan? Master These Essential Phrases! 🇯🇵✨

Whether you're hunting for the perfect outfit or just browsing, these Japanese phrases will make your shopping experience a breeze!

1. Do you have this in stock?
   
    🗣️ これの在庫はありますか？
    
    Kore no zaiko wa arimasu ka?
    
2. Do you have this in different colors?
    
    🗣️ これの色違いはありますか？
    
    Kore no iro chigai wa arimasu ka?
    
3. Do you have this in size M?
    
    🗣️ これのMサイズはありますか？
    
    Kore no M saizu wa arimasu ka?
    
4. Where is the fitting room?
    
    🗣️ 試着室はどこですか？
    
    Shichakushitsu wa doko desu ka?
    
5. Where is the checkout?
    
    🗣️ お会計はどこですか？
    
    Okaikei wa doko desu ka?
    

🔹 Practice these phrases before your trip
🔹 Don’t be shy to ask for help
🔹 Enjoy your shopping adventure!

snippet.title: How do you like to drink coffee?☕✨ #learnjapanese #jlpt #japaneselanguage
snippet.description:
📖✨ Learn Japanese with Coffee! ☕🇯🇵 ✨📖

 Here are some useful phrases! 👇

🌟 砂糖なしで（お願いします）
/satou nashi de (onegaishimasu)/
 → Without sugar, please.

🌟 砂糖を入れてください
/satou o irete kudasai/
 → Please add sugar.

🌟 牛乳なしで（お願いします）
/ gyuunyuu nashi de (onegaishimasu)/
 → Without milk, please.

🌟 牛乳を入れてください
/gyuunyuu o irete kudasai/
 → Please add milk.

🌟 ブラックで（お願いします）
/burakku de (onegaishimasu)
 → Black, please.

🌟 アイスで（お願いします）
/aisu de (onegaishimasu)/
 → Iced, please.

🌟 ホットで（お願いします） 
/hotto de (onegaishimasu)/
→ Hot, please.

🌟 カフェイン
/kafein/
 → Caffeine

🌟 ノンカフェイン
/non kafein/
 → Decaffeinated / Caffeine-free

🌟 豆乳 
/tounyuu/
→ Soy milk

snippet.title: Casual vs Formal🔥 (Adverb part.2) #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
@am_japanese 

✅ Really / Truly
Casual: 本当に (hontō ni)
Formal: 誠に (makoto ni)

✅ As expected / After all
Casual: やっぱり (yappari)
Formal: やはり (yahari)

✅ Gradually
Casual: だんだん (dandan)
Formal: 徐々に (jojo ni)

✅ Sometimes / Occasionally
Casual: たまに (tama ni)
Formal: 時折 (tokiori)

✅ Most / The most
Casual: 一番 (ichiban)
Formal: 最も (mottomo)

✅ All / Everything
Casual: 全部 (zenbu)
Formal: 全て (subete)

How many did you know?!😎
I hope it’s helpful😊👍

snippet.title: How many ways to say "I" in Japanese? Check the captions,too😎✨ #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
🗣️ Did you know there are many ways to say "I" in Japanese? 🇯🇵

Full version is on my instagram: https://www.instagram.com/reel/C_XzzLWMEfl/

Each expression reflects the speaker's personality, gender, and even the context. Here’s a quick breakdown:

🔸 **Watashi (私)**: Neutral and polite. Commonly used by everyone in formal situations.

🔸 **Boku (僕)**: Often used by men, especially younger ones or those who want to sound modest or humble.

🔸 **Ore (俺)**: Masculine and casual, used by men in informal settings.

🔸 **Jibun (自分)**: Casual and often used in sports or anime, especially in team settings to emphasize self-responsibility.

🔸 **Uchi (うち)**: Primarily used by women, especially in certain regions of Japan, to casually refer to themselves.

🔸 **Using your own name (自分の名前)**: Some people, especially children, refer to themselves by their own name to sound cute or informal.

And that’s not all!

There are many more ways to express "I" in Japanese, each with its own unique flavor. 🌸 

おいら、おら、俺様、わて、おら

あたし、あっし、あたい、わたし、わたくし、うち、我、吾輩、拙者、当方

etc…

Choosing the right one can express who you are! 🌟 

Understanding these nuances can deepen your appreciation of Japanese culture and language.

Follow for more Japanese contents🗻

▶︎ @am_japanese_sushi_ramen

For more Japanese contents😉

✅ Correct
Textbook: 正解です (seikai desu)
Casual:　そ (so)

✅ Really？
Textbook: 本当ですか？ (hontou desuka)
Casual: そう？ (sou)

✅ Yes
Textbook: そうですね (soudesune)
Casual: そうそう (sousou)

✅ That's right
Textbook: その通りです (sono toori desu)
Casual: そうそうそう (sousousou)

✅ That’s what I wanted to say
Textbook: まさにそれです (masani sore desu)
Casual: そうそうそうそうそうそう (sousousousousou)

Let’s talk like a native speaker!haha
I hope it’s helpful😊👍

Ｄon’t forget to save this video to review later😉

snippet.title: How many "たつ(tatsu)" did you know?!😎🔥 #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage #jlpt
snippet.description:
How many「たつ (tatsu)」did you know!?😎

Did you know 「たつ (tatsu)」has many meanings in Japanese?😲
It's not just “立つ (stand up)”!

Let’s explore its different uses!🙌

1⃣🧍‍♂️ 立つ (stand up) /tatsu/
ex.椅子から立つ = stand up from a chair
/isu kara tatsu/

2⃣⏳ 経つ (time passes) /tatsu/
ex. 時間が経つ= Time passes
/jikan ga tatsu/

3⃣🚭 断つ (quit) /tatsu/
ex. お酒を断つ= Quit drinking
/osake o tatsu/

4⃣🏠 建つ (be built) /tatsu/
ex. 家が建つ= a house is built
/ie ga tatsu/

5⃣🧳 発つ (depart, leave) /tatsu/
ex. 旅に発つ= leave for a trip
/tabi ni tatsu/

🔤 All these are pronounced “tatsu” but mean totally different things!

📚 Japanese is full of fun and surprising expressions—let’s keep exploring together!😉🇯🇵💪


🍣🍜🌸🍵🗻👘

snippet.title: 言 is N5 kanji BUT...😲‼️#kanji #learnajapenese #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯

言 has many different readings in Japanese😎

⭐ i(u)

✅ 言う（い-う / i-u）
📝meaning: To say
ex.)ありがとうと言う (arigatou to iu)
＝To say thank you

⭐koto
✅ 一言（ひと-こと / hito-koto）
📝meaning: a word, a few words
ex.)一言いい？ (hitokoto ii?)
=Can I have a quick word?

✅ 言葉（こと-ば / koto-ba)
📝meaning: a word
ex.)丁寧な言葉で話す(teinei na kotoba de hanasu)
=speak in polite language

⭐gen
✅ 名言（めい-げん / mei-gen)
📝meaning: a favorite quote
ex.)一番好きな名言はこれです。(ichiban sukina meigen wa kore desu)
=This is my most favorite quote.

✅ 言動（げん-どう / gen-dou)
📝meaning: words and actions
ex.)言動が一貫していない。(gendou ga ikkan shitenai)
=There’s a mismatch between his words and actions.

⭐gon
✅ 伝言（でん-ごん / den-gon)
📝meaning: pass the word
ex.)伝言を残す。(dengon o nokosu)
=leave a message
ex.)伝言を預かる（dengon o azukaru)
=take a message

✅ 無言（む-ごん / mu-gon)
📝meaning: silence,without a word
ex.)彼は急に無言になった。(kare wa kyuuni mugon ni natta)
=He suddenly fell silent.

I hope it’s useful💪🔥

snippet.description:

zettai iku
(100%)

iku iku
(90%)

✅ 多分いける！ → I can probably make it!
tabun ikeru
(70%)

✅ まだわからない → I’m not sure yet.
mada wakaranai
(50%)

✅ 予定確認してみるね → I’ll check my schedule.
yotei kakunin shitemirune
(40%)

✅ 行けたら行く → I’ll go if I can.
iketara iku
(20%)

✅ その日はちょっと… → That might be difficult…
sono hi wa chotto..
(10%)

✅ ごめん、無理 → Sorry, I can’t.
gomen muri
(0%)

It may be different for each person but it's quite like this I guess😂
I hope it’s useful 😊👍

snippet.title: 5 "かく(kaku)" in Japanese🇯🇵 How many did you know?! #learnjapanese #jlpt #kanji
snippet.description:
Checkout the captions below to learn more!✨

Did you know there are so many ways to use 「かく (kaku) 」 in Japanese?

1⃣✍️字を「書く」
じを「かく」
=to write
/ji o kaku/

2⃣🎨絵を「描く」
えを「かく」
=to draw
/e o kaku/

3⃣💸資金を「欠く」
しきんを「かく」
=to lack funds
/shikin o kaku/

4⃣頭を「掻く」
あたまを「かく」
=to scratch one’s head
/atama o kaku/

5⃣🏊水を「掻く」
みずを「かく」
=to paddle/stroke the water)
/mizu o kaku/

🍣🍜🌸🍵🗻👘

snippet.title: Similar sound, big oops!🤣⚠️ #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
🚨 Japanese vs Spanish🚨 

Words that sound the same but mean totally different things! 🤔🌏 
Let’s explore these fun “false friends” between Spanish and Japanese! 

☑casa（house)🏠 sounds the same as 傘(かさ-umbrella)☔️

☑Que？(What?) sounds the same as 毛(け-hair)

☑ajo (garlic) sounds like あほ(stupid)

☑vaca (cow) sounds the same as 馬鹿(ばか-stupid)

☑Chinchín (Cheers🍻)　sounds like ちんちん (man’s 🍆）

When you're learning new words, these are always the ones you somehow remember first, don’t you think?? lol

snippet.title: ＼Textbook vs Casual🔥／
snippet.description:
Ｄon’t forget to save this video to review later😉
For more Japanese contents!
@am_japanese

✅ Buy online
Textbook: オンラインで買う (onrain de kau)
Casual: ポチる (pochiru)

✅ Copy and paste
Textbook: コピーして貼り付ける (kopii shite haritsukeru)
Casual: コピペする (kopipe suru)

✅ Make a mistake
Textbook: 間違える (machigaeru)
Casual: ミスる (misuru)

✅ Google search
Textbook: グーグルで検索する (guuguru de kensaku suru)
Casual: ググる (guguru)

✅ Be obsessed with / Hooked on
Textbook: 夢中になる (muchuu ni naru)
Casual: ハマる (hamaru)

✅ Skip class or work
Textbook: なまける (namakeru)
Casual: サボる (saboru)

✅ Steal / Copy an idea
Textbook: 盗む (nusumu)
Casual: パクる (pakuru)

✅ Go viral
Textbook: 流行る (hayaru)
Casual: バズる (bazuru)

How many did you know?!😎

snippet.title: Textbook vs Lazy Japanese😁 #learnjapanese #jlpt #nihongo
snippet.description:
＼Textbook vs Lazy Japanese😁／

For more Japanese contents
👉 @am_japanese 

✅ Yes
Textbook: はい(hai)
Lazy: うん (un)

✅ No
Textbook: いいえ(iie)
Lazy: ううん (uun)

✅ What should I do...?
Textbook: どうしようかな...(dō shiyou ka na)
Lazy: うーん... (uuun...)

✅ I agree.
Textbook: そうですね(sō desune)
Lazy: うんうんうんうん (unununun)

✅ I’m in trouble.
Textbook: 困りました(komarimashita)
Lazy: う... (u...)

✅ What?
Textbook: なんですか？(nandesuka?)
Lazy: ぅん？ (un?)


How many did you know?!😎
I hope it’s helpful😊👍

snippet.title: 6 different "kakeru" in #japanese part.1😎👍✨ #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt

and more…!!!

snippet.title: 火🔥 is N5 kanji BUT...🤯 #kanji #jlpt #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
火(fire) has many different readings in Japanese😎
​ @am_japanese 

⭐hi

✅ 火（ひ / hi）
📝meaning: big

✅ 火花(ひ / hi-bana)
📝meaning: spark

✅火蓋（ひ-ぶた / hi-buta）
📝meaning: trigger; the moment something begins (lit. “fire lid” of a matchlock gun)

✅火種（ひ-だね / hi-dane）
📝meaning: embers; spark; source of trouble or conflict

⭐bi
✅ 花火 (はな-び / hana-bi)
📝meaning: firework

✅ 強火（つよ-び / tsuyo-bi）
📝meaning: high heat / high flame

✅ 中火（ちゅう-び / chuu-bi）
📝meaning: medium heat

✅ 弱火（よわ-び / yawa-bi）
📝meaning: low heat

⭐ka
✅ 火力（か-りょく / ka-ryoku）
📝meaning: firepower; heating power; energy output

✅火力発電（か-りょく-はつ-でん / ka-ryoku-hatsu-den）
📝meaning: thermal power generation; electricity produced by burning fuel

✅ 火災（か-さい / ka-sai）
📝meaning: fire; blaze（fire disaster)

✅ 火曜日（か-よう-び / ka-you-bi）
📝meaning: Tuesday

⭐ho
✅火影（ほ-かげ / ho-kage）
📝meaning: firelight; shadows or shapes that appear in flickering firelight

⭐bo (special reading)
✅小火（ぼや / boya)
📝 meaning: small fire; minor fire incident

⭐yakedo(special reading)
✅火傷（や-けど / ya-kedo）
📝meaning: burn (injury)

かんじっておもしろいね！
みんながんばろう！💪🔥

氷（こおり）の量（りょう）は、「少（すく）な目（め）」
=Less ice, please.
-koori no ryoo wa sukuname

トッピングはパールで。
=Topping with tapioca pearls,please.

- toppingu wa paaru de.

はい、以上（いじょう）です。
=OK, that's all.
-hai, ijoo desu.

💁‍♀️袋（ふくろ）はご利用（りよう）ですか？
=Would you like a plastic bag?
-fukuro wa goriyoo desuka?

👩🏻袋（ふくろ）はなしで大丈夫（だいじょうぶ）です。
=I don't need a bag, thanks.
-fukuro wa nashi de daijoobu desu.

💁‍♀️1,770円です。
=It's 1,770yen.
-sen nana hyaku nana juu en desu.

👩🏻はい。
=OK
-hai

Suicaで支払(しはら)いお願（ねが）いします。
=I'd like to pay with Suica, please.
-suica de shiharai onegaishimasu.

💁‍♀️98（きゅうじゅう）番（ばん）でお待（ま）ちください。
=Please wait for number 98.
-kyuujuu hachi ban de omachi kudasai

💁‍♀️98（きゅうじゅう）番（ばん）です。
=It's number 98.
-kyuujuu hachi ban desu.

💁‍♀️お待(ま）たせしました。
=Thank you for waiting.
-omatase shimashita

チョコレートミルクと、黒糖（こくとう）ミルクと、グレープミルクティーです。
chocoreeto miruku to kokutoo miruku to gureepu miruku tii desu.

👩🏻ありがとうございます。
-Thank you.

👧おいしい！
-Yummy!
-oishii

And, let's make sure to separate the trash properly👍✨

🌟飲(の)み残(のこ)し-nomi nokoshi
=Leftover drink

🌟プラスチック-purasuchikku
=Plastic

snippet.title: We learn this kanji earlier but one of the difficult kanji to read☺️ #learnjapanese #nihongo #kanji
snippet.description:
"日" has many ways of reading!
How many of them do you know?✨😎

日（ひ）-hi
ex)日付(ひづけ)-date

日（び）-bi
ex)曜日(ようび)-day of the week

日（にち）-nichi
ex)日常生活（にちじょうせいかつ）-daily life

日（か）-ka
ex)三日（みっか）-3 days

日（じつ）-jitsu
ex)祝日（しゅくじつ）-public holiday

Special readings
今日（きょう）-Today
明日（あした/あす）-tomorrow
明後日（あさって/みょうごにち）-asatte/myougonichi
一日（ついたち/いっぴ）-tsuitachi/ippi

.

snippet.title: How many ways to say "dad" in #japanese do you know?🇯🇵😎 #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
🗣️ Did you know there are many ways to say "Dad" in Japanese? 🇯🇵

🔸 お父さん (Otousan): Neutral, commonly used by everyone in daily conversations to refer to their father.

🔸 父上 (Chichiue): Extremely formal and respectful, often used historically or humorously nowadays, giving a dignified feeling.

🔸 パパ (Papa): Casual, affectionate, and often used by young children or those who prefer a softer, warmer tone.

🔸 あじゃ (Aja): A special dialect from Tokunoshima, used affectionately to refer to one's father, highlighting regional warmth and tradition.

🔸 おとう (Otou): Casual and intimate, frequently used within close-knit families to express familiarity and warmth.

🔸 父ちゃん (Touchan): Casual and friendly, giving a cozy, approachable feel, often used affectionately in casual family conversations.

🔸 パピ、パピ子 (Papi,Papiko): Playful, slightly humorous or teasing way to call one's father, indicating a close, fun relationship.

Have you heard any of these before?
Which one do you like the best?

But guess what—there are even more ways to call your father in Japanese! 🌸
おやじ、とと、おとん・・・and more!

Follow for more Japanese contents 🗻


#Japanese #LearnJapanese #JapaneseCulture #JapanLover #LanguageLearning #LanguageLovers #Nihongo #JapaneseLanguage #JapanOfInstagood #ExploreJapan #JapaneseLife #JapanTravel #JapaneseGrammar #日本語 #日本 #言語学習

snippet.title: Casual vs Formal🔥at the restaurant/store #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
Don’t forget to save this post to review later😉
For more Japanese content🍣🍜
@am_japanese

✅ How many people?
Casual: 何人？ (nannin?)
Formal: 何名様ですか？ (nanmeisama desu ka?)

✅ This way, please.
Casual: こっちこっち (kotchi kotchi)
Formal: こちらへどうぞ (kochira e douzo)

✅ Please have a seat.
Casual: 座って (suwatte)
Formal: おかけください (okake kudasai)

✅ Please take a look.
Casual: 見て (mite)
Formal: ご覧ください (goran kudasai)

✅ What would you like to order?
Casual: どうする？ (dou suru?)
Formal: いかがなさいますか？ (ikaga nasaimasu ka?)

✅ Please wait a moment.
Casual: ちょっと待って (chotto matte)
Formal: 少々お待ちください (shoushou omachi kudasai)

✅ OK.
Casual: わかった (wakatta)
Formal: かしこまりました (kashikomarimashita)

✅ I’m sorry.
Casual: ごめん (gomen)
Formal: 申し訳ございません (moushiwake gozaimasen)

✅ Please don’t do that.
Casual: それやめて (sore yamete) / やめてください (yamete kudasai)
Formal: ご遠慮ください (goenryo kudasai)

✅ Please come again.
Casual: またきてね (mata kite ne)
Formal: またお越しください (mata okoshi kudasai)


How many did you know?!😎
I hope it’s helpful😊👍

snippet.title: Just one hiragana changes the whole meaning!😅⚠️ #learnjapanese #jlpt #nihongo
snippet.description:
🚨 Just One Sound Changes the Meaning! 🚨

In Japanese, 
💺座る(すわる-suwaru)=to sit

✋触る(さわる-sawaru)=to touch

Just the difference between
 "す" and "さ" 
completely changes the meaning! 🪑👋

✨ Example:
Can I sit here?
→ ここに座っていいですか？
(koko ni suwatte iidesuka?)

Can I touch this?
→ これを触っていいですか？
(kore o sawatte iidesuka?)

Mastering these little differences will help you sound more natural in Japanese! 😉🇯🇵

snippet.title: How manw "つける(tsukeru)" do you know?!✨😎 #learnjapanese #kanji #jlpt
snippet.description:
Check the caption below😎👍✨

All sentences below use 「かける(nuku)」
but has different meanings!😲

①To Add
味を「つける」
/aji o “tsukeru”/
=To add flavor

音をつける
/ oto o “tsukeru”/
=To add sound

②To Apply
化粧水を「つける」
/keshousui o “tsukeru”/
=To apply toner

日焼け止めを「つける」
/hiyakedome o “tsukeru”/
= To apply sun screan

③To mark,grade
丸を「つける」
/ maru o “tsukeru”/
=To circle the correct answer

成績を「つける」
/ seiseki wo “tsukeru”/
=To grade

④To follow, tail
後を「つける」
/ ato o “tsukeru”/
=To tail someone

I hope it’s usefull😉👍

snippet.title: Scary?!😱⚠️Japanese Idioms!! #learnjapanese  #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:

☑ 目を盗む (Me wo nusumu)
= To do somthing behind someone’s back

目(め)＝eyes
盗(ぬす)む= To steal

☑ 口が滑る (Kuchi ga suberu)
= To slip the toungue (actually similar to Japanese😲）
＜Direct Translation＞
口(くち)=mouth
滑(すべ)る=To slip

☑ 首を切られる (Kubi wo kirareru)
= To be fired
＜Direct Translation＞
首(くび)=neck
切られる=To be cut

snippet.title: "明" is N5 kanji BUT...😎🤯 #kanji #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
明 has many different readings in Japanese😎

✅明るい：aka (rui)
=Bright

ex.)
◾️この明るい色はあなたによく似合います。
（この あかるい いろ は あなた に よく にあいます。）
This bright color really suits you.

✅明ける：a (keru)
=①To dawn ②to begin ③to end

ex.)
①夜が明ける。
（よるがあける。）
Dawn breaks.

②新年が明けました。
（しんねんがあけました。）
The New Year has begun.

③梅雨が明けると、夏が来る。
（つゆがあけると、なつがくる。）
When the rainy season ends, summer arrives.

✅明らか：aki (raka)
=clear, obvious

ex.)
◾️原因（げんいん）が明（あきら）かになった。
The cause became clear.
◾️彼（かれ）は明（あき）らかに嘘（うそ）をついている。
He is clearly lying.

✅明：mei
◾️説明（せつ”めい”ーsetu “mei”）
=explanation
◾️明確（”めい”かくー”mei”kaku）
=clear

✅明日：ashita／asu／myounichi
=Tomorrow
＜NOTE📝＞

🌟あした (ashita) — Casual / spoken; the most commonly used in everyday conversation.
Example: 明日（あした）行くよ。 — I’ll go tomorrow.

🌟あす (asu) — More polite / somewhat formal; often seen in business emails and announcements.
Example: 明日（あす）お伺いします。 — I will visit (you) tomorrow.

🌟みょうにち (myōnichi) — Very formal / literary; used in official announcements, reports, and news headlines.
Example: 明日（みょうにち）開催されます。 — It will be held tomorrow.

✅明：myou
◾️明日（”みょう”にち-”myou”nichi）
=tomorrow
◾️明星（”みょう”じょう-”myou”jou）
=morning star, rising star

✅明：min
◾️明朝体（”みん”ちょうたい- “min”choutai）
=Japanese serif font

みんながんばろう！💪🔥

#Japanese #LearnJapanese #JapaneseCulture #JapanLover #LanguageLearning #LanguageLovers #Nihongo #JapaneseLanguage #JapanOfInstagood #ExploreJapan #JapaneseLife #JapanTravel #JapaneseGrammar #日本語 #日本 #言語学習

snippet.title: 小 is N5 kanji and easy to write BUT for reading...🤯 #jlpt #japaneselanguage #kanji
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯

小(small) has many different readings in Japanese😎

⭐chii-sai
✅ 小さい（ちい-さい / Chii-sai)
📝meaning: small

⭐Shō

✅ 小（しょう / Shō）
📝meaning: small

ex.)小学校(しょう-がっこう-Shō-gakkō)
=elementary school

⭐o
✅ 小川（お-がわ / o-gawa）
📝meaning: Stream

⭐ko
✅ 小人 (こ-びと / ko-bito）
📝meaning: Little person/dwarf

⭐azuki(special reading)
✅ 小豆 (あずき-azuki)
📝meaning: Red beans

⭐sa 
✅ 小夜 (さ-よ / sa-yo)
📝meaning: It’s an old-fashioned, poetic way to say “night.”
小夜 (Sayo) is also used as a female given name in Japan.


みんながんばろう！💪🔥
@am_japanese

snippet.title: Casual vs Formal Part.3 Verbs🔥 #learnjapanese #jlpt #nihongo
snippet.description:
＼Casual vs Formal🔥／
Part.3 Must-Know Daily Verbs💪

✅ Use
Casual: 使う (tsukau)
Formal: 使用する (shiyou suru)

✅ Buy
Casual: 買う (kau)
Formal: 購入する (kounyuu suru)

✅ Sell
Casual: 売る (uru)
Formal: 販売する (hanbai suru)

✅ Start
Casual: 始める (hajimeru)
Formal: 開始する (kaishi suru)

✅ Finish
Casual: 終わる (owaru)
Formal: 終了する (shuuryou suru)

✅ Decide
Casual: 決める (kimeru)
Formal: 決定する (kettei suru)

✅ Change
Casual: 変える (kaeru)
Formal: 変更する (henkou suru)

✅ Leave
Casual: 出る (deru)
Formal: 出発する (shuppatsu suru)

✅ Arrive
Casual: 着く (tsuku)
Formal: 到着する (touchaku suru)

✅ Write
Casual: 書く (kaku)
Formal: 記載する (kisai suru)

How many did you know?!😎
I hope it’s helpful😊👍

Don’t forget to save this post to review later😉
For more Japanese content🍣🍜
@am_japanese

snippet.title: Summer Tradition in Japan🎆👘🍧✨ #japan #japaneseculture #japantravel #learnjapanese
Level of delicious/ not delicious 😋🤮🍽️ #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage  #jlpt
snippet.description:
From “meh” to “OMG”🍽
Let’s learn real food expressions!🇯🇵✨

When it’s delicious😋💓
【Lv.1】まぁまぁ(maa maa)
=Not bad.

【Lv.2】イケる(ikeru)
=Actually good.

【Lv.3】美味しい（oishii）
=It’s good.

【Lv.4】うまっ (uma)
= It’s Soo good.

【Lv.MAX】やばっ (yaba)
~It’s insane!🔥

When it’s bad taste😖
【Lv.1】微妙（びみょう-bimyou）
=Meh…

【Lv.2】美味しくない(oishikunai)
=It’s not good.

【Lv.3】まずい (mazui)
=It tastes bad.

【Lv.4】まずっ (mazu)
=Yuck

【Lv.MAX】やばっ (yaba)
=Disgusting!

I hope it’s useful😊👍


#learnjapanese #studyjapanese #nihongo #japaneselesson #jlpt

snippet.title: What's the difference between 「なしで」and 「ぬきで」？💡🤔 #learnjapanese #jlpt #japaneselesson
snippet.description:
＼Important👀‼／
What's the difference between
 「ぬきで」 and 「なしで」? 💡

Both can be translated as  "without,"
but there's a subtle difference! 🚀

🌟なしで（nashide)= Not adding
=It means "without something that was never there."
 You don’t add anything.
Example: Drink coffee without milk (milk wasn’t in it at first).

🌟ぬきで（nukide)= Taking away
=It means "without something that is usually there.
"You take it out or skip it”.
Example: Order a burger without pickles (pickles is usually part of the burger).

Master these small nuances,
and your Japanese will sound more natural!😉🌸

Thank you for watching!

#japanese #japaneselesson #japaneselanguage #learnjapanese #studyjapanese #nihongo #jlpt #japanesegrammar #日本語 #日本語学習

snippet.title: Change the intonation, change the meaning🫨 #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
Change the intonation, change the meaning! 🔊

So here, 

When saying "OK," raise the pitch at the end of “いいよ↗（iiyo)."

When saying "No thank you," lower the pitch at the end of "いいよ↘(iiyo)."

＜Script＞
👩：一緒（いっしょ）にカフェ行（い）かない？
=Wanna go to a cafe together?

👩🏻：いいよ↗
=Sure

👩：やった～
＝Yay!

【At the cafe】

👩：これ、美味（おい）しい～！
=This tastes so good!
食（た）べる？
=Wanna eat some?

👩🏻：あ～..いいよ↘
=Ummm, No thank you.

👩：じゃあ、はい！
=Then,here you go.

👩🏻：ん？いらないよ
=Eh? I mean, I don't want it.

👩：え？でも、いいよって言（い）った
=Eh? But you said “iiyo”.

👩🏻：いいよ↗は、OKってこと。
=iiyo↗(raise the pitch at the end) means OK.

いいよ↘は　No thank youってこと。
=iiyo↘(lower the pitch at the end) means No thank you.

👩：日本語（にほんご）難（むずか）しい‥‥
=Japanese is so hard…

- - - - - 

Save this post so that you can review later🔖 And follow for more to learn Japanese♪ ▶

[@am_japanese_sushi_ramen](https://www.instagram.com/am_japanese_sushi_ramen/)

🍣🍜🌸🍵🗻👘

snippet.title: Standard Japanese vs Tokunoshima dialect🏝️🇯🇵 #learnjapanese #nihongo #japanese
snippet.description:
▼Interesting Facts!🔍▼

It's toooootally different, right?haha
The Tokunoshima dialect is one of the Amami languages and has been recognized by UNESCO as one of the endangered languages😢
It's sad to lose such a unique and wonderful language. So, in my town, as an effort to preserve the dialect, there are active initiatives where elderly people teach the dialect to young children.

I was raised by my grandmother, so while I can't speak the dialect, I can understand it! 👍

Oh, I shouldn't forget to say this, too!
Tokunoshima is also registered as a UNESCO World Natural Heritage site, and it is a place of incredible natural beauty as well ✨.

🌟Hello
【Standard】こんにちは（konnichiwa)
【Tokunoshima】きゅううがめーら(kyuu ugameera)

🌟Thank you
【Standard】ありがとう(Arigatoo)
【Tokunoshima】おぼらだれん(oboradaren)

🌟I'm sorry
【Standard】ごめんなさい(Gomennasai)
【Tokunoshima】しめらん(shimeran)

🌟How are you?
【Standard】元気(げんき)？(genki?)
【Tokunoshima】きまいあーむぃ(kimai aamui)

🌟See you
【Standard】またね(matane)
【Tokunoshima】またや／しかや(mataya／shikaya)

#Kanji #LearnJapanese #studyjapanese #japaneselesson #nihongo #jlpt

snippet.title: Lost in Translation... #japanese #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
Ever mixed up “konsento” (outlet/socket) and “consent”? 😂🔌
Things got a little out of hand when we tried to “connect”... quite literally! 😅 haha

【Key Words】
☑ コンセント (konsento)=outlet/socket
☑ 挿す (sasu)=put into/insert
☑ コンセントに挿す（konsento ni sasu）=plug into the outlet
☑ 刺す（sasu）=stab🔪

In this skit, there were two funny misunderstandings!
① コンセント (konsento) sounds like the English word “consent,” but they have totally different meanings.
② 挿す (sasu) and 刺す (sasu) have the same pronunciation, but very different meanings! 😁

【Script】
👩🏻ベアちゃん、コンセントある？
（Hey, Bea！Do you have a "konsento(outlet)"?）

👩🏼 え？consent? My consent?

👩🏻 うん！コンセント！
(Yes! konsento!)

👩🏼 For what?

👩🏻  え？これさしたいんだけど
（e? I want to plug this in... )

👩🏼What?! stab me with the plug!? 😨
No way!!!

👩🏻え～さすだけなのに
(eeee I just want to plug this in)

👩🏼Are you phyco？！

👩🏻お願い！ちょっとだけ！
(Please! Just for a little bit!!)

👩🏼Nooooooo（run away)

👩🏻「コンセントに挿す」だけなのに…なんで！？
(I just wanted to plug it into the outlet…why was she upset!?)

snippet.title: 石 is N5 Kanji BUT...💥🤯 #kanji #learnjapanese #japaneselanguage
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
石(stone) has many different readings in Japanese😎

⭐ishi
✅ 石（いし / ishi）
📝meaning: stone

⭐seki
✅ 化石（か-せき / ka-seki）
📝meaning: fossil

✅ 石油（せき-ゆ / seki-yu)
📝meaning: oil

✅ 石炭(せき-たん / seki-tan)
📝meaning: coal

✅ 宝石（ほう-せき / hou-seki)
📝meaning: jewelry

⭐iwa
✅ 石見（いわ-み / iwa-mi）
📝meaning: Iwami (place name in Japan)

⭐koku
✅ 一万石（いちまん-ごく / ichiman-goku）
📝meaning: 10,000 koku (unit of wealth in old Japan)

⭐shaku
✅ 磁石（じ-しゃく / ji-shaku）
📝meaning: magnet

⭐special reading
✅ 流石（さすが / sasuga）
📝meaning: as expected; impressive

日本語の漢字って、おもしろいね！🥺
みんながんばろう！💪🔥

snippet.title: How many "とる(toru)" did you know?😎 #learnjapanese #jlpt #kanji
snippet.description:
How many「とる (toru)」did you know!?😎

Did you know 「とる (toru)」has many meanings in Japanese?😲
It's not just “取る (To take something)”!

Let’s explore its different uses!🙌

1⃣ 📸 撮る /toru/ （＝to take a photo)
ex. 写真を撮る = take a picture
/shashin o toru/

2⃣ 🥛 取る  /toru/ (=to pick up(or take) something)
ex. コップを取る = pick up a cup
/koppu o toru/

3⃣ 🍎 摂る  /toru/ (=to take in)
ex. 栄養を摂る =To take in nutrients
/eiyō o toru/

4⃣ 🎣 捕る /toru/ (=to catch typically used for animals, fish, insects, etc.)
ex. 魚を捕る = catch fish 
/sakana o toru/

5⃣ 🎒 盗る  /toru/ (=to steal)
ex. 駅で他人のカバンを盗る = steal someone else’s bag at the station
/eki de tanin no kaban o nusumu/

There are more like

✅執る（toru)=To take on a role, office, duty
✅録る（toru）=To record
✅獲る（toru）=To ”seize” or “to capture,” especially in the sense of catching or hunting animals, fish, or game. It emphasizes obtaining something by effort or skill (often in the wild).

🔤 All these are pronounced “toru” but mean totally different things!

📚 Japanese is full of fun and surprising expressions—let’s keep exploring together!😉🇯🇵💪

🍣🍜🌸🍵🗻👘

#japanese #japaneseLanguage #learnJapanese #japanesevocabulary #japanesewords #studyJapanese #nihongo #japaneselessons #日语 #日本語学習 #日本語勉強中 #JLPT #日本語能力試験

snippet.description:
🎤 Special Interview #11 Highlights：
📝 Learning Japanese Abroad & in Japan — Chris shares how he first started studying Japanese overseas, how he kept learning after moving to Japan, and the daily habits that helped him reach the N2 level.
🏆 Journey to the Speech Contest — Hear how Chris prepared for and won the Japanese Speech Contest, plus his advice for learners who want to express themselves more naturally and confidently.
🏠 Life in Japan — Chris talks about his real daily life in Japan — work, cultural differences, and the mix of challenges and happy moments he’s discovered while living here.
🎧 Practice Tip — Shadow the conversation to improve your rhythm and intonation, and feel the natural flow of real spoken Japanese.

snippet.title: Japanese is SO EASY?!!!😎　#learnjapanese #studyjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
✨Japanese is　SO EASY!?😲✨

Did you know you can hold a conversation in Japanese with just ONE word? 👍

✅ I got it.
Polite: 了解です (Ryoukai desu)
Casual: りょ (Ryo)

✅ And then?
Polite: それで？ (Sore de?)
Casual: で？ (De?)

✅ I totally agree.
Polite: ですよね～ (Desu yo ne~)
Casual: ね～ (Ne~)

✅ Wait a moment.
Polite: ちょっと待ってください (Chotto matte kudasai)
Casual: ちょ ちょ ちょ ちょ (Cho cho cho cho)

💬 Which one do you want to try first?
Let me know in the comments below!👇

snippet.title: N5 kanji BUT...🤯 #learnjapanese #kanji #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
上 has many different readings in Japanese😎

☑ ue
上(うえ-ue)–Top, above

☑ jou
”上”手 (”jou”zu)–be good at sth

☑ uwa
”上”手 (”uwa”te)-upper part

🈳 kami
”上”半期 (”kami”hanki)-First Half of the year

☑ a
”上”がる (”a”garu)-to go up

☑ nobo
”上”る (”nobo”ru)-to walk up

【Note📝】上がる, 上る difference

Use 上がる for things rising.

Use 上る for physically going up somewhere.

Examples:
＜上がる＞
- 気温が上がる。= The tempereture goes up.
(kion ga agaru)
- 値段が上がる。= The price goes up.
(nedan ga agaru)

＜上る＞
- 階段を上る。= Climb the stairs.
(kaidan o noboru)
- 煙が立ち上る= Smoke billows up
(kemuri ga tati noboru)


#LearnJapanese #studyjapanese #japaneselesson #nihongo

snippet.title: How many "かける(kakeru)" did you know?!😎✨ #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
Checkout the captions too for more to learn！✨

Did you know 「かける(kakeru)」 has multiple meanings? 
Let’s dive into its versatile uses!🙌

1⃣🎵 音楽（おんがく）をかける (play music) /ongaku wo kakeru/

2⃣🧹 掃除機（そうじき）をかける (vacuum cleaning) /soujiki wo kakeru/

3⃣🪄 魔法（まほう）をかける (cast a spell) /mahou wo kakeru/

4⃣😖 迷惑（めいわく）をかける (cause trouble) /meiwaku wo kakeru/

5⃣🛌 布団（ふとん）をかける (put a blanket on) /futon wo kakeru/

and more…!!!

📚 Keep learning and exploring the richness of the Japanese language together!😉 🇯🇵💪

🍣🍜🌸🍵🗻👘

snippet.title: It is said that over 400 expressions for rain☔️😯 #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
Did you know!? It is said that Japanese has over 400 different expressions for rain!!!🤯

Here are some of the expressions that I used in the video!👍

☑梅雨（つゆ:tsuyu）=rainy season
☑大雨(おおあめ:ooame)=heavy rain
☑ゲリラ(げりら)豪雨(ごうう):gerira gouu=sudden downpour
☑通(とお)り雨(あめ):toori ame=passing shower
☑鉄砲雨(てっぽうあめ):teppou ame=flash rain
☑霧雨（きりさめ:kirisame）=Drizzle
☑小雨(こさめ:kosame)=light rain
☑にわか雨(にわかあめ:niwakaame)=shower
☑狐(きつね)の嫁(よめ)入(い)り:kitsune no yomeiri=天気雨＝sun shower
☑天気雨(てんきあめ:tenkiame)=sun shower

＜Script＞
👩🏻梅雨(つゆ)やばいね。
今日（きょう）、大雨（おおあめ）らしいよ。
＝The rainy season is terrible.
I heard it's going to rain heavily today.

👩昨日のゲリラ豪雨もすごかったね。
通り雨で良かったけど。
市内は、鉄砲雨だったみたい。
=Yesterday's sudden downpour was also intense.
I'm glad it was just a passing shower.
It seemed to be a flash rain in the city.

👩🏻今日、帰る時には
霧雨か小雨くらいで終わってくれないかなー
=I hope it's just a drizzle or light rain when I leave today!

👩確かに。
一昨日みたいに
にわか雨で、狐の嫁入りみたいな
天気雨で終わってくれないかな。
=Indeed.
I hope it's just a shower like the day before yesterday and it ends with sun shower like a fox's bridegroom's wedding.

👩🏻わかる
=Totally.

I hope you find it interesting😉👍
.

snippet.title: In English we say "Never Give Up"! In #japanese literature we say: #learnjapanese #jlpt #nihongo
snippet.description:
Check out the captions, too👍✨

In English we say: Never give up🔥
In Japanese Literature, we say

🌟どんなに長(なが)いトンネルでも限(かぎ)りがある。
（donnani nagai tonneru demo kagiri ga aru）
必ずトンネルを抜(ぬ)ける日(ひ)が来(く)る。
（kanarazu tonneru o nukeru hi ga kuru)
=Even the longest tunnel has an end,A day will come when you will emerge into the light.

🌟壁(かべ)は、その高(たか)い壁(かべ)を越(こ)えられる人(ひと)にしかやってこない。
（kabe wa sono takai kabe o koerareru hito ni shika yattekonai）
=A wall appears only to those who can climb its heights.

🌟朝顔(あさがお)の花(はな)が咲(さ)くには、
(asagao no hana ga saku niwa)
朝(あさ)の光(ひかり)に当(あ)たる前(まえ)に、
(asa no hikari ni ataru mae ni)
夜(よる)の冷気(れいき)と闇(やみ)に包(つつ)まれる時間(じかん)が不可欠(ふかけつ)なんです。
(yoru no reiki to yami ni tsutsumareru jikan ga fukaketsu nandesu)
=For the morning glory to bloom,
It must first be embraced by the night's chill and darkness
before it meets the morning light.

If you like this video, leave 💓 in the comment😊

#japanese #learnjapanese #nihongo #studyjapanese #jlpt #kanji #famousquotes

snippet.title: Textbook vs Japanese slang 😎🔥 #learnjapanese #nihongo #jlpt
snippet.description:
＼Textbook vs Slang🔥／

✅ I’m tired.😫
Polite: 疲れました。(tsukaremashita)
Slang: だる (daru)

✅ I'm OK👍
Polite: 大丈夫です。(Daijōbu desu)
Slang: いける いける (ikeru ikeru)

✅ It’s funny.🤣
Polite: 面白いですね (omoshiroi desu ne)
Slang: ウケる (ukeru)

✅ To cancel at the last minute
Polite: 予定を＜ギリギリで＞キャンセルする (yotei o ＜girigiri de＞kyanseru suru)
Slang: ブチる (buchiru)

✅ Someone you especially like or favor. 🥺💕
Polite: 特に好きな人 (tokuni sukina hito)
Slang: 推し (oshi)

How many did you know?!😎
I hope it’s helpful😊👍

snippet.title: 月 is N5 Kanji BUT...🤯 #kanji #learnjapanese #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯
月(tsuki) has many different readings in Japanese😎

🌙 tsuki

✅ 月（つき / tsuki）
📝meaning: moon

✅ 一月（ひと-つき / hito-tsuki）
📝meaning: one month (duration)

🌙 gatsu
✅ 一月（いち-がつ / ichi-gatsu）
📝meaning: January

🌙 getsu
✅ 月曜日（げつ-よう-び / getsu-you-bi）
📝meaning: Monday

🌙 kurage（Special Reading）
✅ 海月（くらげ / kurage）
📝meaning: Jellyfish

🌙 Kisaragi（Special Reading）
✅ 如月 (きさらぎ / kisaragi）
📝meaning: 2nd month of the lunar calender

🌙 tsuku
✅ 月読（つく-よみ / tsuku-yomi）
📝meaning: the Shinto god of the moon
(literal meaning: moon reading)
"Tsuku" is an older or compound reading of "tsuki" (moon); in modern Japanese, it's usually read as "tsuki.”

You might know this word if you like Naruto😉🔥🥷

みんながんばろう！💪🔥

Real: ううん (uun)

Real: わかんない (wakannai)

Textbook: とても (totemo)
Real: めっちゃ (meccha)

Textbook: ハンサム (hansamu)
Real: イケメン (ikemen)

✅ Not delicious
Textbook: おいしくない (oishikunai) 
Real: まず (mazu)

✅ I agree / Exactly
Textbook: そうですね (sou desu ne)
Real: それな (sorena)

ex.)

/ shira ga/

/ shira yuki hime /

- - - - - - - - - 

⭐ haku
ex.)
■白紙=Blank Paper
/ haku shi /

■白鳥=Swan
/ haku chou /

- - - - - - - - - 

⭐ byaku
ex.)
■三百円=300 yen
/ san byaku en /

■白夜=the midnight sun/white night
/ byaku ya /

/ sa yu /

snippet.title: N5 kanji "空" but...🤯🤯🤯 #learnjapanese #kanji #jlpt
snippet.description:
N5 Kanji with many readings!??🤯

空 has many different readings in Japanese😎

☑ sora
🛣️空(ひと-sora)–sky

☑ kara
👛空っぽ(からっぽ-karappo)–empty

☑ kuu
🈳 空席(くうせき-kuuseki)-vacant seat

☑ su(iteiru)　
🚃空いている(すいている-suiteiru)–hungry, less crowded

☑ a(iteiru)
💺空いている(あいている-aiteiru)–available,open

🔥Reading Challenge🔥

空(??)がきれいで
(The sky was beautiful,)

空(??)気が澄んでいる日だった
(and the air was clear.)

お腹が空(??)いていたので、
(I was hungry,so)

ちょうど席が空(??)いていたレストランに入った。
(I went into a restaurant that happened to have an open seat.)

たくさん食べて財布が空(??)っぽになった。
(I ate a lot, and my wallet ended up empty.)


みんながんばろうね！💪🔥
